data.title: Cathie Wood predicts 'Goldilocks' boom in 2026: 5% GDP with deflation, calls Bitcoin a 'ultimate diversifier' for portfolios.
data.description: ARK Invest CEO Cathie Wood predicts an economic "Goldilocks" scenario in 2026 with real GDP growth exceeding 5% and potential deflation, as AI-led productivity booms. Wood states the US economy is leaving a three-year "rolling recession," with real GDP growth surpassing 4% in late 2025. She believes this growth is driven by technology and productivity, not inflation. Wood anticipates declining oil prices, potentially down by 20-25%, and diminishing unit labor costs, suggesting inflation could turn negative. She asserts that productivity-driven growth leads to falling inflation, contrary to consensus fears. Wood's full analysis is on Benzinga.com.

data.title: Sidetrade achieves SOC 1® Type II, SOC 2® Type II reports, and ISO 27001 certification, showcasing control maturity and data protection.
data.description: Sidetrade, a leading Order-to-Cash AI firm, revealed it has achieved SOC 1 ® Type II and SOC 2 ® Type II certifications with a clean auditor's opinion. This was confirmed after evaluations by EY France and a successful ISO/IEC 27001:2022 recertification. These endorsements underline the company's high level of control maturity, robust data protection measures, and operational strength at a significant level. Given the rising need for credible assurances regarding AI governance and financial process integrity, notably in regulated sectors, this accomplishment is crucial.

data.title: Enrique Patrickson appointed new CFO of Hexagon AB, taking office by July 2026, succeeding interim CFO Norbert Hanke.
data.description: Hexagon AB, based in Stockholm, has disclosed the selection of Enrique Patrickson as the Chief Financial Officer (CFO) of the company. Patrickson is set to assume the role no later than July 2026, taking over from the current interim CFO, Norbert Hanke. This change marks a significant development for Hexagon AB and its financial leadership team.

data.title: SKF to release Q4 2025 results on 30 January at 07:30 CET. Audio webcast at 09:00 CET. Open to investors, analysts, and media.
data.description: In Gothenburg, Sweden, SKF is scheduled to release its Q4 results for 2025 on January 30th around 07:30 CET. An audio webcast in English will follow at 09:00 CET on the same day. Investors, analysts, and media professionals can participate in the webcast by logging in at the provided platform.

data.title: Trump insists Big Tech like Microsoft cover data center power bills to prevent burdening Americans, no specifics revealed.
data.description: President Donald Trump revealed on Monday that his administration is collaborating with prominent U.S. tech firms, especially Microsoft Corporation (NASDAQ: MSFT), to address the issue of data center power consumption impact on Americans. In a statement on Truth Social, Trump expressed his concern by stating, "I never want Americans to face increased electricity costs due to data centers." He emphasized that the U.S. leads in AI and identified data centers as crucial for this advancement but stressed that tech companies should cover their own expenses. Trump hinted at forthcoming "significant adjustments" to be disclosed starting this week to ensure that Americans are not financially burdened by the power consumption of these data centers. The full article is accessible on Benzinga.com.

data.title: INVL Renewable Energy Fund I repaid EUR 17.5 million loan to Cordiant Capital early, 6 months ahead of schedule.
data.description: RENEWABLE ENERGY FUND I managed by INVL Asset Management, a prominent alternative asset management group in the Baltics, disclosed that their subsidiary, REFI Solar, successfully repaid a significant EUR 17.5 million loan to Cordiant Capital, a global investment management firm. This repayment was made almost six months earlier than the initial maturity date, showcasing REFI Solar's commitment to financial obligations and prudent financial management strategies.

data.title: Litgrid's Board names Andrius Šemeškevičius as CEO of the company; part of EPSO-G group with legal entity code 302826889.
data.description: A new energy company known as EPSO-G has been recently established. With a legal entity code of 302826889, EPSO-G is based in Vilnius, Lithuania, at Laisvės pr. 10.
